programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part one of two. Dr Sloan and Amanda help the FBI investigate a plane crash near the hospital, but just as they think the case is solved, Jesse announces two bodies have gone missing. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.desc: Part two of two. Mark recalls an incident from 20 years previously that appears to hold the key to solving the case involving cover-ups, radioactive fuel and the death of an airline rep. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents examine the death of a seaman and find themselves working alongside Barrett. Gibbs makes his displeasure about the circumstances of the case well known, prompting the rest of the investigators to worry that he might be about to leave the NCIS again. Meanwhile, Ziva introduces her new boyfriend to her team-mates. Crime drama, guest starring Enrique Murciano

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A couple are taking in the view from a cliff top when a car driven by an already bleeding man crashes into theirs and teeters on the brink before plunging over the precipice. Callen and the team are brought in to investigate when the driver is identified as Lieutenant Brian Roth, and they discover that the car is registered to a Hollywood socialite Aubrey Darva. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard Kimble jeopardises his own freedom to treat the critically ill infant of an unwed mother

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A misfiring missile, an inquiry and death on a firing range lead Perry to the defence of an Air Force officer court-martialled on a murder charge

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part story, the entire team is put in danger while hunting for the killer of a former NCIS agent. Jenny Shepard calls in Mike Franks for help, afraid she too is a target - and convinced Gibbs will be next. American crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Bootleggers kidnap two successful country musicians in an effort to profit from the royalties of their hits, and the Rangers must rescue them as thoughts turn to murder

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While transporting the corpse of Lt Gordon Roth to the morgue, a flat tyre brings Ducky and Jimmy to a halt and they are abducted and taken to a remote cabin - where one of their captors wants the post-mortem to take place. Back at the scene, Abby finds a blood sample that does not belong to the victim, who in fact bore little resemblance to the picture of Roth in his old school yearbook. Guest starring Valerie Cruz (The Following)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Eric pulls the team together when he finds a video of kidnapped agent Dominic Vail posted on a jihadist website, with the terrorists holding him stating that he will be executed within 24 hours unless a comrade of theirs being held by the US government is released. One of the captors is identified as a being an associate of Sam, and his involvement also seems to indicate Dom is being held in Sudan. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Vance sticks rigidly to the rules as he investigates the death of a fellow agent, but Gibbs and Franks take matters into their own hands to catch the killers. programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon tries to climb the social ladder - but ends up meeting a snake. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A student at Rod's school is reluctant to talk about who wounded him with a knife, but some dogged routine work by Reg and Diane searching through CCTV footage eventually gives the team a lead, linking the incident to the disappearance of a paroled prisoner last seen stealing bandages from a chemist's shop. The sight of Gina in her glad rags prompts June to wish for early retirement - and an early wedding. Leslie Grantham guest stars

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Michael pleads with Donna to lie for him as Annalese pursues the truth about his fidelity. Linden finds evidence of Jac's scheming but struggles to decide what to do, while Chrissie tries to get Daisha out of Mark's life. Ric's zero-tolerance programme is challenged by the offer of a charitable donation to the hospital, and Elliot's decision to bring his dog to work again backfires when it knocks Jac over. Melanie Hill and David Roper guest star

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Abs tries to get through to an increasingly unstable foster child who has harmed herself with a broken bottle because of her fear of turning out like her alcoholic mother. Charlie's first custody hearing is adjourned after Louis discovers his grandad fighting for breath, and Comfort agrees to Fin's suggestion that they see a relationship counsellor, while Lara shares a kiss with Simon on his return to work

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Smithy's wish for a quiet birthday is soon forgotten when Kezia announces she's got a party planned and he finds himself dodging bullets on the Aldbourne estate. Newly promoted DI Sam Nixon is put firmly in her place by Neil and promptly goes behind his back to revive the search for missing youngster Amy Tennant on the anniversary of her disappearance. June tells Tony and Reg she's taking early retirement

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Michelle has some unfinished business to attend to, Cindy has to do some quick thinking but soon realises she hasn't been rumbled, and Arthur has a welcome surprise

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: The past catches up with Ted, Pauline crosses swords with Michelle, and Roy is out for Barry's blood

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Regina threatens Sarah's future as George's life hangs in the balance, while Anna and Gino's honeymoon is clouded by the contents of Swanson's letter

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Tom fears cyclist Nick Pullen is being pushed beyond his limits by an overzealous trainer, while Will clashes with ex-wife Kate over an alcoholic patient _ who leaves him with a bloody nose. Sam's mounting debts tempt him to defraud the partnership as his wedding day draws near. Gray O'Brien and Simon Shepherd star

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: The Herriot household becomes inceasingly hectic, Tristan contemplates putting his bachelor days behind him, and Siegfried's new assistant arrives - bringing his badger with him. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: A new season begins, but Jeffrey appears to have vanished. Classic comedy, starring Ruth Madoc and David Griffin

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: With no sign of Jeffrey Fairbrother, Ted is summoned to Head Office, and jumps to the conclusion that he is about to be made entertainments manager. Classic comedy, starring Paul Shane

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth forces Richard to participate in a trip to the countryside to look for Iron Age remains, but further cultural excursions are curtailed when Daisy and Onslow report that Rose is behaving in an outrageous fashion. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Smiler finds himself pursued by a wanton gold-digger who thinks he's rich, but luckily Truly is full of ideas to solve the problem. Stephen Lewis and Frank Thornton star

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are hired to create a restaurant garden on Italy's Ligurian coast, but it is not long before their working holiday is disrupted by a murder. Determined to solve the crime before returning home, they find themselves with no shortage of suspects - and matters are complicated further by a second death. Guest starring Suzanne Bertish (Rome) and Tristan Gemmill (Casualty), with Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: Long-buried secrets are revealed, putting Strike and Robin in danger as they close in on Lula's killer. Robin receives key information from a salesgirl at the boutique Lula visited, Strike asks Bristow about Lula's attempts to trace her biological roots and the impact on her adoptive family, while John points out that he, Tony and Yvette all have an alibi. However, Strike manages to unravel Tony's alibi as the truth about Lula's death is finally revealed. Tom Burke and Holliday Grainger star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Pilot episode of the crime drama, starring Amanda Redman as Superintendent Sandra Pullman, a rising star of the Met who is sidelined after a failed hostage rescue. Put in charge of a new department, she is dismayed to discover the unit is to be manned by a motley crew of old-school former detectives who know little about modern police procedure. However, when they re-examine the case of a man whose conviction for murder 20 years ago has just been overturned, their combined experience and unorthodox methods bring interesting results. Alun Armstrong, Dennis Waterman and James Bolam play Redman's veteran team, with Jon Finch, Tim Woodwork and Philip Bretherton

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The team races to stop a CIA plan to assassinate the Venezuelan president in Britain in the hope of gaining intelligence about an expected al-Qaeda attack on a school. Having identified the group of mercenaries that kidnapped Zaf, Jo too is snatched by them. Adam's attempts to gain her freedom result in a high-level conspiracy being exposed - and more tragedy for the Section D officers

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Smithy's wish for a quiet birthday is soon forgotten when Kezia announces she's got a party planned and he finds himself dodging bullets on the Aldbourne estate. Romantic drama, starring Brett Climo and Marta Dusseldorp

programme.desc: A wild dog causes havoc in the market at Skeldale, Tristan is charged with examining a consignment of sheep bound for Russia, and James acquires a new record player. Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison star

programme.desc: The opening day of the season arrives and staff gather to welcome the first guests at Maplin's holiday camp. Comedy, starring Paul Shane, Sue Pollard and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!

programme.desc: Hyacinth and Richard take over a country cottage for a couple of days, with unexpected results. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.desc: Barry and Glenda get a shock when a man they foolishly exchanged addresses with on holiday turns up on their doorstep. To make a strained situation worse, it appears their guest has an unusual hobby

Fortunately, the guest list includes Miss Marple, who immediately sets about tracking down the killer. Feature-length drama, starring Joan Hickson, Claire Bloom and Barry Newman

programme.desc: Pilot episode of the crime drama, starring Amanda Redman as Superintendent Sandra Pullman, a rising star of the Met who is sidelined after a failed hostage rescue. programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: A British soldier is kidnapped by al-Qaeda, with the warning he will be killed unless Remembrance Day services are cancelled. Harry is reunited with his protege Lucas North, who has spent eight years in a Russian prison, while another agent who was sent to that country is uncovered and must make a dangerous journey home. Drama, starring Peter Firth and Richard Armitage

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Henry VIII is desperate to end his marriage to Katherine of Aragon, but Cardinal Wolsey's efforts to persuade the pope to grant an annulment are fruitless - much to the dismay of Thomas Cromwell, a lawyer dependent on Wolsey's patronage. The king's new mistress is rumoured to be Anne Boleyn, who has sworn vengeance on Wolsey after he refused to let her marry Harry Percy, so Cromwell visits her in the hope of swaying her to the cardinal's side. Historical drama adapted by Peter Straughan from Hilary Mantel's Booker Prize-winning novels Wolf Hall and Bring Up the Bodies, and starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Claire Foy and Jonathan Pryce

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Emma works out that abductor Shelley Fitzgerald is likely to take Amy Tennant to her childhood haunts in Brighton. programme.desc: Ted finds Clive's classic car gathering dust, and comes up with a devious money-making scheme, in which he raffles it as a prize, but he switches it for an old banger before delivering it to the lucky winner. Classic comedy, starring Paul Shane and David Griffin

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth makes expensive vacation plans and discovers that voluntary church cleaning is certainly no holiday. Meanwhile, Rose ends up in a compromising position. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Mary Millar

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Alvin tries to persuade Nora Batty to go for a ride in his sidecar, but she's having none of it. When she relates the story of her long-standing feud with Audrey Craig - who had the audacity to laugh at her hat during a wedding many years ago - it becomes clear the broom-brandishing battleaxe is not one to be messed with

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detectives' case against a violent criminal is thrown into jeopardy by the murder of a key witness. Determined to secure a conviction, they immerse themselves in the seedy underworld of a seaside town, and as they press for answers, Havers is brought face to face with her childhood memories. Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team members have to draw on their powers of detection and discretion when a painting in the Queen's private collection is found to be a fake - and all the clues lead to an art forger and political activist who seemingly committed suicide after a fire destroyed his studio. Meanwhile, prospective grandfather Standing has to sell his beloved Triumph Stag to buy his pregnant daughter a pushchair. Anthony Head guest stars alongside Dennis Waterman and Amanda Redman

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Harry vows revenge on Kachimov following the explosion, but the government demands proof of Russian involvement. Lucas is desperate to return to the Grid, but doubts remain about where his loyalty lies. However, when Ros's efforts to blackmail an oil tycoon produce few results, it seems North may be needed after all. Drama, starring Peter Firth, Hermione Norris and Richard Armitage

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Emma works out that abductor Shelley Fitzgerald is likely to take Amy Tennant to her childhood haunts in Brighton. Lewis is the first to congratulate her for getting the hunt back on track, but husband Matt continues to belittle everything she does. Neil leads the team in a frantic search on the Sussex coast, where an elated James Tennant finally sets eyes on his daughter for the first time in a year

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Anne Boleyn summons Cromwell, anxious to know whether she has an ally in him, and he witnesses her venting her frustrations on her ladies-in-waiting. The king starts to take notice of Cromwell and comes to rely on the lawyer's advice, but then news arrives from York that Cardinal Wolsey has been arrested by Harry Percy, a vengeful act for denying his betrothal to Anne eight years earlier. Historical drama adapted from Hilary Mantel's Booker prize-winning novels Wolf Hall and Bring Up the Bodies, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Claire Foy and Jonathan Pryce

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Cromwell manoeuvres a bill through Parliament acknowledging Henry as head of the Church of England, the first step in enabling the king to grant his own divorce from Katherine of Aragon. The Boleyn family also looks to the lawyer to deal with Harry Percy, whose wife claims her own marriage is unlawful on the grounds that her husband had previously made a binding contract of marriage with Anne Boleyn. This is a task Cromwell approaches with relish, remembering how Percy helped bring down Wolsey. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis and Claire Foy

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin head to properties in Chesterton in Staffordshire, Hornchurch in Essex and Sudbrook in Monmouthshire to hear the buyers' plans

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Questions are asked when a year after a house fire a second claim comes in, while the data embedded in digital photographs submitted to support stolen items does not add up

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of the army of night workers who toil through the small hours to get the world ready for when the rest of the population awakes. In the first edition, vets perform potentially life-saving surgery on a much-loved horse, and bus cleaners work to have Newcastle's transport ready for commuters

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks are in Leominster, Herefordshire, to help two teams look for items, while presenter Christina Trevanion learns about local photographer Derek Evans

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al comes up with a plan to get out of the engagement, but Lily has some news of her own. Jimmi is called to the scene of a death and soon finds it has far-reaching consequences

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple searching for a house in rural North Yorkshire on a budget of £800,000, and also visits Harrogate for afternoon tea and to sample the region's traditional sweet treats

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph salvages three items from Earlswood Recycling Centre in Surrey, then she and two experts transform four dining chairs, a pair of wardrobe doors and a retro cabinet

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell takes on Tim Medhurst as they search for items on either side of the border between England and Scotland on their way to an auction in Edinburgh

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich set about transforming a sea of dingy gravel and paving for a couple eager for a peaceful haven. Owners Andrew and Dawn also want a design that allows the family caravan to be accommodated. Whose design will fall on stony ground?

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A surprise call from Lauren in New Zealand leaves Peter asking his dad for financial help - can Ian redeem himself? Linda is surprised to find Mick at home nursing a bruised eye, while he tries to cover his displeasure when Max talks about helping her with her new business venture. Kheerat warns Tina about moving in with Gray, claming he is not who people think he is

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: First episode of Richard Curtis's comedy, starring Dawn French as the new vicar of a sleepy English village. Her arrival causes consternation among the locals - particularly stuffy councillor David Horton, who vows to have her ejected at all costs. Gary Waldhorn, James Fleet and Emma Chambers co-star

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Journalist Josh Baker investigates the story of former Indiana resident Samantha Sally and her family. After arriving in Syria, her husband became an Islamic State sniper and her young son Matthew was forced to take part in an infamous propaganda video. Sally, who is now in prison, describes her life with the terror group and the part she played in buying sex slaves

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: The staff of Longlox are reunited as they prepare to re-open the salon after lockdown, and while things are going well with Gary, Sammyjo is feeling the pressure to get everything in place for her waiting clients

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: The salon opens for its first weekend after lockdown, Gary pays busy Sammyjo a visit, and sad news at home affects Holly's replacement

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Richard Osman and Fin Taylor joining Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Hugh Grant discusses his starring role in psychological thriller The Undoing, while TV cook and food writer Nigella Lawson talks about her new book Cook, Eat, Repeat. Hollywood stars Jamie Foxx and Tina Fey reflect on their involvement in new Disney animation Soul and six-time Oscar-nominated Amy Adams talks about starring in the latest film from Ron Howard, Hillbilly Elegy. Plus, Dua Lipa performs Levitating

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the stories of a quirky property in Fulham, a huge renovation in Tamworth, Staffordshire, and a terrace in Bradford

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: An amateur detective turns the tables on fraudsters running a crash-for-cash scam, and fur flies as a woman claiming her pedigree cat has been stolen is caught out by social media

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: The staff of Newcastle's Theatre Royal has to get one show out and another one in, while AA mechanics come to the aid of stricken motorists and Care Call deals with emergency cases

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross looks after proceedings as Danny Sebastian and David Harper help two teams find items in Dorking in Surrey, hoping to make a profit at an auction in Maidenhead

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Bear has two unexpected encounters, while Valerie competes with Emma for Aashiq's attention during cookery class. Ruhma has a challenging day under the watchful gaze of Doreen

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a house-hunting couple with a £900,000 budget move back from France and find an impressive rural retreat in the Norfolk countryside

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore restyles a bed frame found at the recycling centre in Woking, while experts find a creative new use for some metal ladders and reupholster a small armchair

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Timothy Medhurst search for bargains in Perthshire. Phil samples some scones in Scone, while Tim shows off his skills on an antique piano

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A furious Peter punishes Bobby for their dad's decision to take him out of his will, just as Ian is being questioned by a local journalist - and deflecting any tricky questions by divulging other people's dodgy dealings. Gray lets his facade slip in front of his children and Tina tells Mick she has a plan to make things up to him. Callum tells DI Thompson he is close to locating the source of the 999 call - but his discovery comes at a huge cost

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Max wrestles with the after-effects of neurosurgery and the financial pressure on the hospital, but things get worse when an encounter with an old enemy puts a strain on her relationship with Louis. Kian brings in some new recruits to help solve Jac's medical mystery, and a new arrival stirs up unpleasant memories for Donna

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Adrian Edmondson, Claude Littner, Cariad Lloyd and Jordan Stephens to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The chefs are challenged to fillet and smoke a trout for their Skills Test, and serve the results with celeriac remoulade. Their next task is to make a classic crepe suzette, a dish that requires extreme precision, while in the final round, they must impress judges Marcus Wareing, Gregg Wallace and Monica Galetti with their very own two course signature dishes

programme.title: We Are Who We Are
programme.desc: Drama about an American teenager who is forced to relocate to a military base in Italy when his mother is placed in charge. As he struggles to fit in with his strange new surroundings, he finds himself drawn towards a mysterious girl. Starring Jack Dylan Grazer and Chloe Sevigny

programme.title: We Are Who We Are
programme.desc: Caitlin wrestles with society's standards of femininity as she has her first period, and finds her relationship with her father becoming increasingly strained. When she leaves the house dressed as a boy to go and hang out in the local bar, Fraser follows her to find out what she gets up to

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore properties up for auction in Farningham, Kent, Great Barr in Birmingham and Crewe in Cheshire, then discuss the owners' plans

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A fraudster feigns innocence when caught scamming a holiday company. Elsewhere, CCTV shows that an employee claiming compensation was actually drunk when he fell

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: Workers undertake major road repairs that must be completed before morning rush hour, and one of the North East's biggest bakeries toil through the night baking fresh products

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams o Liverpudlians take on the antiques challenge in Cheshire, with the help of experts Ben Cooper and Richard Madley. As usual they have one hour to buy three items to take to auction, but also have the added pressure of a shopping challenge chosen by host Eric Knowles. On top of that, one of their items must cost at least £75

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen and Rob get an emergency call from Social Services. Meanwhile, Valerie's jealousy starts to get out of hand

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple hoping to find literary inspiration and seaside serenity for their £500,000 budget on the Dorset coast, and also meets two printmakers

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore items found at Walsall Recycling Centre, including a pair of metal gates and a set of mid-century chairs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Tim Medhurst tour the byways and waterways of the Cotswolds as they search for items to sell for a profit at auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Ronan Keating

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: With sky high property prices, home ownership seems out of reach to many. The government's shared ownership scheme is supposed to help get people on the property ladder by letting them buy part of their home and rent the rest, but this programme hears from shared owners who say uncapped service charges, building defects and costly lease extensions have left them stuck in homes with escalating costs and huge debts

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Silversmith Brenton West takes a look at a brass replica fire engine, while Andrew Gorrie from Lancashire hopes leatherwork expert Suzie Fletcher can save a fragile leather wallet with an extraordinary history. Elsewhere, the experts set to work repairing a handmade radio system and a banana barrow

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four more professional chefs arrive from across the nation to compete, first taking on the infamous Skills Test and recreating dishes set by Marcus and Monica. The first two chefs try their hand at Marcus's challenge of preparing scallops with a romesco sauce and a marinated tomato salad, before the second pair of competitors prepare and cook lambs' kidneys and serve on toast with a Madeira mustard sauce for Monica. In the second round, the four chefs must impress the judges with two stand out Signature Dishes of their own creation, before two of the competitors are sent home

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: DJ Target, Krept and Konan set two new challenges for the artists. First they must create a beat, before shooting a music video

programme.title: Step into the Ring
programme.desc: Documentary following aspiring young wrestlers from Norwich. Pocket tries to overcome her anxiety and get back in the ring, and Marcus sets out to prove that anyone can become a pro if they are determined enough

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the sixth episode, couple Melissa and Daniel from Dartford face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with two sets of buyers whose rural aspirations were driven by a spirit of adventure. She first heads to the Welsh Borders for a reunion with a couple who managed to buy their first home - a rural farmhouse with space to accommodate their many hobbies and dogs - in 2017. Nicki then moves on to Devon's south coast to revisit a couple of surfing enthusiasts who, after reassessing their priorities, have successfully built a new life by the sea with their young son

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore uses her salvaging expertise at Witley Recycling Centre to search for three items that she can clean up and reinvent. To help her, she calls on trusty makers Leigh-Anne Treadwell and Duncan McKean. Leigh-Anne takes on two wingback armchairs in need of a makeover, while lighting expert Duncan has some retro lampshades to contend with. Sarah herself sees potential in an extendable dining table

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Jennie drives into the mountains to help with a case that reminds her that being a Mountain Vet is anything but glamorous, as she has to lance a giant boil the size of a watermelon on the leg of a cow. Julie, Castle Vets' head nurse, has seen most things in her long career, but today is a first for her, as a rare wild animal has been brought in, found lying at the side of the road and before assessing what's wrong with it, she must first find out what it is

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary Berry explores the world of simple comfort food, demonstrating how to make heartwarming, fuss-free dishes that can warm the cockles and lift the spirits. In the second episode, Mary takes a journey along the Thames in London to discover culinary hidden gems that inspire her to make some deliciously comforting dishes. Her journey starts with the Henley Rowing Club, where she rustles up mixed bean and butternut squash wraps for the team, before heading to The Waterside Inn, the world famous three Michelin Star restaurant run by the Roux family. Later, Mary discovers an oasis floating on the Thames, when she meets an established river community who have turned their barges into gardens teaming with fruit and vegetables

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett in which contestants must answer questions from nine categories, so must choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves. The first contestants are a creative writing student, a financial blogger, and a primary school teacher

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: An appeal for funds on behalf of a registered charity

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Arlo still has not told Lindsey about his feelings for Poppy, and the stress of knowing what he is up to is beginning to take its toll on Billy

programme.title: The Big Painting Challenge
programme.desc: Una Stubbs and Richard Bacon host this art competition in which 10 keen amateurs take on three specially designed challenges each week, showing their skills using a wide range of mediums. The contest begins in Alnwick Castle, Northumberland - which may be familiar to fans of the Harry Potter movies as Hogwarts. Here, the contestants demonstrate their styles for the first time, before a quick-draw challenge and a painting of the castle - after which, painters and expert judges Daphne Todd and Lachlan Goudie decide who will be leaving

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: Documentary following the fortunes of humans and animals over the course of a year in Alaska, which is by far the biggest US state and still one of the wildest places on Earth with deep forests and vast mountain ranges. The arrival of spring sees Alaska go through a huge transformation as water, light and warmth return, with animals emerging from hibernation and rushing to cash in on the riches of the season. Dougray Scott narrates

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Michael Baggott assess objects brought into the University of Nottingham, while presenter Paul Martin learns about a Nottingham-born Victorian prize-fighter called Bendigo and meets locals who are keeping his memory alive

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: David James, Rhys James, Denise Lewis and Isy Suttie go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball is joined by Caroline Quentin and Johannes Radebe, the fourth couple to leave the competition after losing out in last night's dance-off to Maisie Smith and Gorka Márquez

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair to answer questions on the specialist subjects of Joe Calzaghe, the Beach Boys, Joseph Stalin, and the Gospel of John, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts as first-round losers the Ticket Collectors and the Walruses get another chance to stay in the competition and reach the second stage. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues comprising Alfonso Valiente, Lionel Richie, Papa, and Antony Kidman

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson prepares a layered chocolate and peanut butter cake, and uses liquorice pellets to make a sauce for a Basque burnt cheesecake. She also demonstrates a recipe for roast vegetables with cumin and fennel seeds to accompany spiced bulgur wheat and beetroot sauce

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: A look at the impact of Covid-19 on the Royal Free Hospital's transplant services, which treat some of the trust's most vulnerable patients and were largely unable to continue during the first wave of the pandemic. Four months after lockdown began, the service was finally able to start again and the department performed three months' worth of transplants in five weeks. But as the hospital begins to see infection numbers rise once again, how long can they continue to offer these life-changing operations?

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon is joined by Russell Kane and Tracy-Ann Oberman to take a light-hearted look at the year's cultural highlights, including the revival of Doctor Who, and Paula Radcliffe winning the London Marathon.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Keeping Britain Fed
programme.desc: Sara Cox and Ade Adepitan gain access to some of Britain's biggest supermarkets and their suppliers to see how they are dealing with the coronavirus outbreak, the most testing time in their history. Sara finds out what goes on in one supermarket over 24 hours, from opening to closing and all through the night, to see how they are coping during the crisis. Elsewhere, Ade finds out how the nations' farms, factories and depots have risen to the challenge to produce and supply our supermarkets with food in these exceptional circumstances

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison explores the powers of the great outdoors to provide a tonic in troubled times as she immerses herself in nature, from forest bathing to wild swimming. There are also films from the Countryfile archives about the health-giving prowess of the UK's green havens, including John Craven finding out how nature helped heal wartime wounds, and Anita Rani visiting a special farm giving respite to refugees

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team descend on Caswell Bay, Swansea, to transform a dilapidated, out-of-use bus shelter into the first, fully adapted surf centre Surfability UK, a charity that provides surfing lessons and experiences for people with disabilities and learning difficulties, and which receives funding from Children in Need. Throughout, Nick presents stories of how children and young people have benefitted from the support of the charity, but the challenge remains tough as this is the team's first build during Covid-19

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham reports live on badger and fox families in the New Forest, while Michaela Strachan catches up with the latest from a seal breeding colony on the Isle of May. Iolo Williams goes in search of a wild parrot-like bird in a secret location in west Wales, and Gillian Burke discovers the surprising wildlife to be found amongst the chimney stacks and industrial ponds of South Yorkshire

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the first heat, four professional chefs from around the country face three challenges to earn a place in this week's quarter final. After the initial test of basic kitchen skills, they must prepare tortellini stuffed with Mushroom Duxelles and served with a sauce of their choice in just 25 minutes. For the final round, they must recreate Marcus Wareing's recipe for devilled butterflied mackerel with herb-flavoured mayo and a cucumber and fennel salad

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: An exploration of insurance fraud, including dishonest claimants being caught on camera

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph salvages three items from Earlswood Recycling Centre in Surrey, then she and two experts transform four dining chairs, a pair of wardrobe doors and a retro cabinet

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook meets four generations of food lovers in Cardiff. While joining the Aslams to rustle up some of their favourite curries, fish dishes and a keema bolognese, Michela meets 90-year-old head of the family Mohammed, who came from Bangladesh in 1958 and went on to open one of Wales' very first Asian restaurants. Michela tries to prise the family's secret spice mix as well as helping grandson Yusuf to master one of his grandmother's specialities to bring along to a special party to celebrate his grandparents' 54th wedding anniversary

programme.title: Powering Britain
programme.desc: Keeley Donovan goes behind the scenes of Drax Power Station, 14 miles from York in North Yorkshire, and the biggest power station in Britain. She follows engineers as they undertake a £50million maintenance programme that requires them to move equipment weighing 300 tons. There are also giant boilers and turbines to refurbish as they get one of their six generating units re-connected to the National Grid

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: A customer advisor, an actor and a retired police officer must choose between combining their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves. Rob Beckett hosts

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Charles Hanson, Melissa Downhill and Anthony Devine look for potentially lucrative bargains in Kendal, with a cocktail shaker among the items chosen

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: After what happened with Poppy and Arlo, George and Woody still are not talking, but Hannah has a radical plan to sort them out

programme.title: The Big Painting Challenge
programme.desc: The nine remaining amateur artists face three challenges in portraiture on London's Southbank. For the opening task, they are given four hours to create pictures of themselves from their reflections. This is followed by a quick-draw where they have 30 minutes to attempt an anatomically accurate sketch that captures the likeness of a stranger. In their last chance to impress before the elimination, there's a four-hour sitting with EastEnders actor Rudolph Walker (Patrick Trueman) and Albert Square legend Pam St Clement (Pat Butcher)

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: The land is bathed in 24-hour sunlight during the short Alaskan summer, luring hummingbirds up from Mexico, exposing deserts in the Arctic and relying on parachuting firefighters to tackle forest fires in its vast wilderness. Grizzly bears fatten up on a sudden wealth of salmon, a caribou calf joins a vast herd on its great migration and a huge male moose finds unlikely ways to impress a female. Among the human population, a dedicated grower of giant vegetables takes his half-ton pumpkin to the Alaska State Fair

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: David Harper and Adam Partridge search for items to sell at auction at Penrhyn Castle in north Wales. Paul Martin uncovers the story of Britain's longest-running industrial strike

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: David James, Rhys James, Denise Lewis and Isy Suttie go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball chats to Maisie Smith and Gorka Márquez, who on Sunday night survived the dance-off for the second week in a row. Plus a closer look at the weekend's performances

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Benidorm stars Sherrie Hewson and Tony Maudsley seek fortune in Leicestershire and Nottinghamshire. Providing guidance are dealer Roo Irvine and auctioneer Tim Medhurst. They'll finish at auction in North Hykeham, but first music lover Tony joins a merry band of toy soldiers in Melton Mowbray. Meanwhile, food lover Sherrie learns about one of the country's oldest biscuits - Grantham Gingerbreads

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Volunteers in Hastings rush to the aid of a surfer, who had sought to take advantage of the huge waves created by a storm at sea, but has now ended up going missing. On the north coast of Cornwall, the crew of St Agnes race to reach three people caught in a treacherous rip current that is pulling them out to sea, while in Scotland, the crew of Invergordon lifeboat is called out when two men are spotted clinging to an oil rig's anchor chain

programme.title: QI XS
programme.desc: A selection of experiments and demonstrations from recent series of the comedy panel show. Host Sandi Toksvig tries to maintain discipline while Alan Davies and his fellow panellists run riot

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: An opportunity emerges for the new recruits to prove their worth as Felim takes time off for personal reasons and Eric looks for others to fill the gap. Harper leaps in with her take on the housing market, and is offered the opportunity to sit in on an important meeting. However, when she also has some boring data entry to do, she resorts to working through the night, a decision that has dire consequences, felt by the entire floor

programme.title: Out of Her Mind
programme.desc: Sara dials up the drama on Lucy and Stefan's wedding day to distract from her own fears, which are only chemicals after all. Final episode of the sitcom, written by and starring Sara Pascoe

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: DJ Iain Lee gets a call on his phone-in show from Mandy, who tells him how she became trapped in a coffin, having been forced into a robbery. Comedy short written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan, with Natalie Cassidy

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Lockdown 1.0 - Following the Science?
programme.desc: An investigation into just how closely Boris Johnson and the Government followed the scientific evidence throughout the early months of the coronavirus pandemic. Scientists at the heart of Britain's response to the virus reveal what happened behind the scenes, and how problems accessing key data caused flaws in the modelling process that meant fighting the virus was an uphill battle from the very beginning

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams all look at the unseen side of autumn from their four separate locations across the country. Tonight, the team share some big news about hedgehogs, and also looks at one of the cutest and strangest marine predators, the bobtail squid

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four more professional chefs arrive from across the nation to compete. In the infamous Skills Test, two chefs will try their hand at Marcus' test of sweetbreads with a rustic red wine sauce, before the second pair take on Monica's gazed sabayon fruit dessert. In the second round, the four chefs must impress the judges with two stand out Signature Dishes of their own creation, before two of the competitors are sent home

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: An amateur detective turns the tables on fraudsters running a crash-for-cash scam, and fur flies as a woman claiming her pedigree cat has been stolen is caught out by social media

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore restyles a bed frame found at the recycling centre in Woking, while experts find a creative new use for some metal ladders and reupholster a small armchair

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Volunteers come to the aid of a family trapped in their car on the causeway connecting the Holy Island of Lindisfarne to the mainland, who face being slowly engulfed by the sea. The crew of one of the RNLI's few inland stations in Ireland assists two capsized kayakers just a few days after Christmas, and on Dorset's Jurassic Coast, a fossil hunter is cut off by a rising tide

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Antiques expert Roo Irvine, trader Danny Sebastian and furniture designer Nessa Doran O'Reilly head to West Kilbride to search for bargains

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George receives the news that his wife Laura's killer may soon be out on parole, while Shay and Siouxsie pick up a mysterious stranger in their car

programme.title: The Big Painting Challenge
programme.desc: The amateur artists travel to Blenheim Palace in Oxfordshire, the birthplace and ancestral home of Winston Churchill. Their first challenge sees them arranging a collection of personal items - from a large yellow hat to a skull - which they must capture on canvas using acrylics. This is followed by a quick 30-minute sketch of a giant chess board, ensuring that light and shadow are just as important as perspective and scale. In their last chance to impress before the elimination, the hopefuls paint the facade of the palace, attempting to capture the drama of its stunning architecture, while Una talks to Churchill's granddaughter Emma Soames

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: To survive the bone-chilling cold, deadly blizzards and darkness of the eight months of an Alaskan winter takes courage, cunning and remarkable endurance. This episode tells the stories of how the tough and resourceful animal and human inhabitants of the windswept mountain peaks, icy tundra and snowbound forests face up to the challenges of the season

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Jonathan Pratt search for items to sell at auction at the Boconnoc estate in the Cornish countryside. Meanwhile, Paul Martin visits the gardens of Caerhays

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: David James, Rhys James, Denise Lewis and Isy Suttie go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball gives all the backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing, and Ian Waite gives his thoughts on the couples' training progress

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Countryfile presenters Charlotte Smith and Tom Heap swap agriculture for antiques as they search Hampshire and Buckinghamshire for bargain collectibles, assisted by auctioneers James Braxton and Phil Serrell. Their final destination is an auction in Winchester - but before that, Tom turns woodworker as he learns the ancient craft of 'bodging' in the Chiltern hills, while Charlotte meets the famous Aylesbury duck - common to the area for 300 years and the inspiration behind Jemima Puddle-Duck

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Lyra the Pomsky moves in with the busy Cuthbertson family in Gateshead, but after the dog chews toddler Ellie's toys, her parent call in Louise to help stop the destruction and restore harmony to the house. In Cardiff, Ailsa has to decide whether she is confident enough to let Chinese crested powderpuff Fabio run free across the beach

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Emily, Margaret and Nancy are all in shock after the violent events of the previous day. Hal is devastated and vows vengeance. Elsewhere, Lucy and Elizabeth fight to keep Golden Square within their grasp. Starring Samantha Morton, Lesley Manville and Liv Tyler

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Ellie Taylor from BBC Two's The Mash Report presents stand-up comedy from London's Hammersmith Apollo, inviting rising star Fin Taylor and the provocative Tez Ilyas to the stage

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. With Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Documentary following the day-to-day realities the NHS faces in unprecedented times, focusing on the issues and challenges that continually dominate the headlines

programme.title: Barack Obama Talks to David Olusoga
programme.desc: Historian David Olusoga is joined in conversation by former American President Barack Obama to discuss his long-awaited memoir, A Promised Land. He also reflects on the volatile racial divide in the US, his steadfast refusal to abandon American ideals, and revealing details of life as a black First Family in the White House

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: The team focus on the incredible journeys made by wildlife in autumn. Megan McCubbin catches up with rare whales in the Scottish Highlands, Iolo Williams reveals the science behind nocturnal mass migration, Chris Packham and Megan light up the New Forest to visualise secret fungal networks, and Gillian Burke learns how ducks support their own team colours

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The four strongest chefs from the first two heats return for the quarter-final, inventing a dish that makes the most of an eclectic variety of spices. They then have to cook two courses for restaurant critics Jimi Famurewa and William Sitwell, who scrutinise the dishes in each and every detail. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Building of Britain
programme.desc: Fred explores the work of the builders responsible for the waterways of Britain. Visiting the Leeds-Liverpool and Bridgewater canals, he explores their industrial history and demonstrates how early pioneers dug the canals and made them watertight, creating some of Britain's finest engineering

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a cozy barn in winter, with the ground blanketed in snow, set against a soft sky

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Dr Bendor Grosvenor and Emma Dabiri investigate a painting by 18th-century artist Joseph Wright of Derby, housed at the Derby Museum. The piece suffered an industrial scale restoration in the 1970s, but can the team peel back layers of modern restoration on the mysterious landscape painting stuck in the Derby vaults to reveal another hidden masterpiece?

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Docudrama telling the tale of one of America's most famous unsolved crimes, which involved a mysterious fugitive, a hijacked aeroplane and a daring mid-air escape. The film brings the stories of four possible suspects to life through candid testimony, archive footage and stylised drama. But who is telling the truth, who is lying and, ultimately, who is DB Cooper?

programme.title: The Marvellous World of Roald Dahl
programme.desc: Documentary making use of archived letters, writings and biographical material to tell the story of the life of Roald Dahl, in his own inimitable voice. From his time as a fighter pilot in the Second World War, through to his work as a spy and his encounters with Walt Disney and Theodore Roosevelt, the programme explores how Dahl's personal experiences may have informed the fantastical tales he would eventually publish, including Charlie and the Chocolate Factory and Matilda. Includes contributions by Dahl's last wife Liccy, his daughter Lucy and biographer Donald Sturrock. Long-term collaborator Quentin Blake also provides new animated illustrations based on the events of Dahl's life

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.desc: The Rev Richard Coles explores the revolutionary science behind Charles Kingsley's 1863 novel The Water Babies. The broadcaster also looks at the impact the book had on social reform in Victorian England, and how its racist viewpoint has affected the author's reputation

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.desc: Actress Samantha Bond examines E Nesbit's Five Children and It, a fantasy story about a group of siblings who discover a creature that can grant wishes. Samantha also meets authors Jacqueline Wilson and Kate Saunders, who have both published updated and re-versioned stories inspired by Nesbit's work

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a cozy barn in winter, with the ground blanketed in snow, set against a soft sky

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Building of Britain
programme.desc: Fred explores the work of the builders responsible for the waterways of Britain. Visiting the Leeds-Liverpool and Bridgewater canals, he explores their industrial history and demonstrates how early pioneers dug the canals and made them watertight, creating some of Britain's finest engineering

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Dr Bendor Grosvenor and Emma Dabiri investigate a painting by 18th-century artist Joseph Wright of Derby, housed at the Derby Museum. The piece suffered an industrial scale restoration in the 1970s, but can the team peel back layers of modern restoration on the mysterious landscape painting stuck in the Derby vaults to reveal another hidden masterpiece?

programme.title: The Marvellous World of Roald Dahl
programme.desc: Documentary making use of archived letters, writings and biographical material to tell the story of the life of Roald Dahl, in his own inimitable voice. From his time as a fighter pilot in the Second World War, through to his work as a spy and his encounters with Walt Disney and Theodore Roosevelt, the programme explores how Dahl's personal experiences may have informed the fantastical tales he would eventually publish, including Charlie and the Chocolate Factory and Matilda. Includes contributions by Dahl's last wife Liccy, his daughter Lucy and biographer Donald Sturrock. Long-term collaborator Quentin Blake also provides new animated illustrations based on the events of Dahl's life

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Building of Britain
programme.desc: The former steeplejack considers building designs of the 19th century. He visits Eastnor Castle in Herefordshire to examine the work of architect Robert Smirke, then travels to St Giles's Church in Cheadle, Staffordshire, to study the Gothic-inspired designs of Augustus Pugin

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints another picture. Today he creates a seascape, with crashing waves and a sky made of a mixture of gold, pink and mauve

programme.title: The 80s with Dominic Sandbrook
programme.desc: Historian Dominic Sandbrook follows up his 2012 documentary series The 70s by moving on to the following decade. He begins by examining how powerful new forces of choice and consumerism radically reshaped British life - a new culture of consumer-driven populism that propelled Margaret Thatcher to victory. He takes in everything from the popularity of Delia Smith and affordable fashions on the high street, to the subcultures of Britain's youth and the crisis of identity that rocked and splintered the political left

programme.title: Thatcher: A Very British Revolution
programme.desc: Margaret Thatcher's victory in the 1979 general election propels her into power as Britain's first female prime minister, but her first two years are characterised by disruption and division. Her first cabinet is split between a small group aligned with her plans for radical change, but the majority are experienced ministers who prefer a more consensual style of politics. She also has to deal with an economy in deep trouble, with inflation rising, unemployment spiralling and public spending growing

programme.title: The Last Days of Anne Boleyn
programme.desc: On Friday, May 19, 1536, Henry VIII's second wife Anne Boleyn became the first British queen to be executed, but the circumstances of her death remain shrouded in mystery and contradiction. Hilary Mantel, Philippa Gregory and David Starkey are among the leading authors and historians sifting through the evidence to determine why Henry had her killed

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: The satirical show Spitting Image began during Margaret Thatcher's government and lasted to the end of John Major's. The puppets themselves became almost as famous as the politicians they represented, and in 2000 were auctioned at Sotheby's. The programme traces where they now reside, and meets the caricaturists, mould-makers, writers, directors and the rest of the team who worked tirelessly to make the series a success. It also investigates what the show got right, what it got wrong, and whether its 17-year absence has left a hole in the modern broadcasting schedule. Includes contributions by Peter Fluck, Roger Law and John Lloyd

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: Ambitious detective Katrin 'Kata' Gunnarsdottir investigates when an ex-drug dealer is found murdered in the old harbour area of Reykjavik, and finds that she does not have as much control of her professional life as she had planned. Icelandic crime drama, starring Nina Dogg Filippusdottir. In Icelandic

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: Detective Arnar Bodvarsson arrives from Oslo to help Kata find a link between the two murders. At the memorial to the latest victim, they catch the prime suspect, who had broken into his father's office. Icelandic crime drama, starring Nina Dogg Filippusdottir. In Icelandic

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints another picture. Today he creates a seascape, with crashing waves and a sky made of a mixture of gold, pink and mauve

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Age of Steam
programme.desc: The development of steam power, from the earliest experiments in the ancient world to modern-day nuclear power stations. Fred travels to Cornwall to investigate the use of early engines to pump water out of the tin mines

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene that shows even a tiny shanty by a pond can be a warm place in the dead of winter

programme.title: The Mayflower Pilgrims: Behind the Myth
programme.desc: Drama-documentary exploring the truth behind the story of William Bradford and the Mayflower pilgrims, whose voyage to America in the early 17th century is celebrated in the US every year as Thanksgiving. The programme explores the circumstances that compelled them to leave Europe and the ugly reality behind the famous story of their arrival in the new world

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: An exploration of Australia's underwater ecosystem, one of the natural wonders of the world and the largest living structure on the planet. In the first of three programmes, marine biologist Monty Halls examines the complex form of the coral reef itself and the wildlife that lives on it, from deadly fish-hunting snails and sharks that can walk on land to fighting corals and parrotfish, which cocoon themselves every night

programme.title: Life Cinematic
programme.desc: The director Sofia Coppola discusses the movies that have influenced her career, including To Die For, Safe and Fish Tank. Throughout the programme, she also reflects on her working methods and the influence her father Francis Ford Coppola had on her early viewing habits

programme.title: Fish Tank (2009)
programme.desc: Angry and rebellious teenager Mia lives with her single mother and sister in an Essex council estate. Suspended from school, she searches for an escape from her claustrophobic existence and becomes attracted to her mother's new boyfriend, driving the family even further apart. Drama, starring Katie Jarvis and Michael Fassbender

programme.title: Frank Lloyd Wright: The Man Who Built America
programme.desc: Jonathan Adams embarks on a journey across the US to examine some of the buildings designed by Frank Lloyd Wright, the architect, interior designer and writer whose pioneering creations are thought to have defined the architectural identity of 20th-century America. Throughout his trek, Adams aims to illustrate how Wright's formative years spent in a Welsh Unitarian community in Wisconsin inspired in him a love of nature, and a desire to question and defy convention that would come to define his signature designs

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are going to Westman Islands, Iceland, where the world’s largest colony of Atlantic puffins gathers every year. Lars loves these tough little birds and explains how, although they spend most of their lives flying out to sea and fishing, they return to land each year to lay their eggs. When the baby birds, also called pufflings, eventually hatch, they fly out towards the ocean. But the light from nearby towns often confuses them about where to go, leading local children and adults to form 'puffin patrols' to rescue the pufflings. Lars is excited to help out but is upset when he discovers Glitch, also a fan of puffins, is already well-known on the islands for his skills as a puffin rescuer. Although Glitch offers him a place in his puffin patrol, Lars is determined to outdo Glitch with a patrol of his own - but when he recruits a clumsy Grimbot, things take a turn for the worse...

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Silversmith Brenton West takes a look at a brass replica fire engine, while Andrew Gorrie from Lancashire hopes leatherwork expert Suzie Fletcher can save a fragile leather wallet with an extraordinary history. Elsewhere, the experts set to work repairing a handmade radio system and a banana barrow

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four more professional chefs arrive from across the nation to compete, first taking on the infamous Skills Test and recreating dishes set by Marcus and Monica. The first two chefs try their hand at Marcus's challenge of preparing scallops with a romesco sauce and a marinated tomato salad, before the second pair of competitors prepare and cook lambs' kidneys and serve on toast with a Madeira mustard sauce for Monica. In the second round, the four chefs must impress the judges with two stand out Signature Dishes of their own creation, before two of the competitors are sent home

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Bethan Rhys Roberts present a weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: DJ Target, Krept and Konan set two new challenges for the artists. First they must create a beat, before shooting a music video

programme.title: Step into the Ring
programme.desc: Documentary following aspiring young wrestlers from Norwich. Pocket tries to overcome her anxiety and get back in the ring, and Marcus sets out to prove that anyone can become a pro if they are determined enough

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the sixth episode, couple Melissa and Daniel from Dartford face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.desc: First episode of Richard Curtis's comedy, starring Dawn French as the new vicar of a sleepy English village. programme.title: This Is Our Story: Inside Hearts
programme.desc: The leagues are suspended due to the pandemic, with promotion and relegation are being determined by points per game, leaving Hearts bottom of the Scottish Premiership. Chair Ann Budge is determined to get her club out of crisis as relegation looms, and resorts to legal action against the SPFL

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Journalist Josh Baker investigates the story of former Indiana resident Samantha Sally and her family. After arriving in Syria, her husband became an Islamic State sniper and her young son Matthew was forced to take part in an infamous propaganda video. Sally, who is now in prison, describes her life with the terror group and the part she played in buying sex slaves

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals

programme.desc: With sky high property prices, home ownership seems out of reach to many. The government's shared ownership scheme is supposed to help get people on the property ladder by letting them buy part of their home and rent the rest, but this programme hears from shared owners who say uncapped service charges, building defects and costly lease extensions have left them stuck in homes with escalating costs and huge debts

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Silversmith Brenton West takes a look at a brass replica fire engine, while Andrew Gorrie from Lancashire hopes leatherwork expert Suzie Fletcher can save a fragile leather wallet with an extraordinary history. Elsewhere, the experts set to work repairing a handmade radio system and a banana barrow

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four more professional chefs arrive from across the nation to compete, first taking on the infamous Skills Test and recreating dishes set by Marcus and Monica. The first two chefs try their hand at Marcus's challenge of preparing scallops with a romesco sauce and a marinated tomato salad, before the second pair of competitors prepare and cook lambs' kidneys and serve on toast with a Madeira mustard sauce for Monica. In the second round, the four chefs must impress the judges with two stand out Signature Dishes of their own creation, before two of the competitors are sent home

programme.title: Northern Ireland on The One Show
programme.desc: Jennie Browne and Joe Lindsay present films from the magazine programme that were made in Northern Ireland, beginning with a report on Peter Sellers' 1971 mission to help bring love and peace to Belfast at the height of the Troubles

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: DJ Target, Krept and Konan set two new challenges for the artists. First they must create a beat, before shooting a music video

programme.title: Step into the Ring
programme.desc: Documentary following aspiring young wrestlers from Norwich. Pocket tries to overcome her anxiety and get back in the ring, and Marcus sets out to prove that anyone can become a pro if they are determined enough

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the sixth episode, couple Melissa and Daniel from Dartford face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Coverage of the statement to the House of Commons by the Prime Minister, Boris Johnson, on the Integrated Review of Security, Defence, Development and Foreign Policy, from Thursday 19 November

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: MPs on the Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy Committee take evidence on the Covid-19 pandemic and its effect on workers with evidence from the business world, think tanks and trade unions, from Tuesday 17 November

programme.desc: The programme focuses on words, stories and songs beginning with mh

programme.desc: Documentary following the residents of Uig on the Isle Of Lewis as they strive to transform a disused radar station on the edge of a cliff into a community centre

programme.title: Na Trads
programme.desc: Robert Robertson presents music from the Trad Awards and from past Trad Winners, with Sheila Stewart, Griogair Labhruidh, Lori Watson and Karine Polwart among others

programme.title: Tarraing nan Tonn (Chasing Waves)
programme.desc: John Murdo MacAulay from Lewis enjoys a fun-filled and revealing journey into the world of surfing on his home island. Cameras capture him through the seasonal changes - from the depths of a bitter Hebridean winter, to the stunning, long summer nights
