programme.desc: Simon O'Brien repurposes an old butcher's block into a coffee table, while Henry Cole restores a pair of go-karts to race against restorer Guy

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Margreeth start to research their new business idea, but can they find the suppliers to help them? Stephanie's big event - her singles night - arrives, while Sophie and James put on a walking tour near their chateau

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton visits West Lothian, where Laura and David are building a traditional Scottish cottage between Linlithgow and the Firth of Forth. These two engineers think they have a blueprint for self-build bliss, which involves Dave tackling nearly everything himself. However, first the weather and then a bundle of joy threaten to undermine their ambitious schedule, pushing Dave to the edge

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Former pop singer Danny Karne welcomes guests to the Pavilion, his fashion and rock 'n' roll-themed hotel in central London, and the sports car parked outside wows his visitors. But the petrol-head's decision to take his fellow hoteliers to a motoring museum goes down badly when one of them reveals he has no passion for cars

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: John Luce and his partner Pat Sutton invite their fellow hoteliers to the Fox and Duck, a country inn in Therfield, Hertfordshire. The local golf club provides the guests with adequate entertainment, and they appear to bond as they share friendly banter and a spot of singing at the end of the night. However, the following morning brings with it a shower disaster

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Dave and Debbie Lang, who run the New Inn in Newbridge-on-Wye, Powys, play host. Dave is a keen pig breeder and owns the on-site butcher's shop as well as running a pub, so he serves some of his award-winning pork products for breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Kerstin and Robert Sharpe, the owners of Westleigh Farm in Somerset, play host, and despite the initial cobwebs found by some of the guests, they are soon won over by the size of the showers, the quality of the milk and the generous helpings for breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Young meerkat parents Huskie and Beagle have a new baby, Junior, but keepers have to step in when he falls gravely ill. Meanwhile, red panda Nima, who lost her partner last year, meets a new mate, Koda, but the pair do not get off to the best start, and in the penguin pool, a new male called Flounder arrives

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge joins Elaine and David as they look around an 1850s chateau with 25 rooms, telling them what to look out for when buying such a vast property. The programme also catches up with Tim and Krys from the first series, who renovated rooms for a boutique B&B in the Dordogne, but have been struggling to juggle parenthood with running the business

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Chris Moller meets Steve Wilson and Wendy Grell, who want to get out of the city and live the quiet life. They've demolished their old family holiday home and are building a permanent abode at a small, isolated Northland bay, three hours' drive from Auckland. Their vision is to construct an architectural showpiece, with angled walls and rooflines clad in rusty weathering steel, but they need their build to blend in in a rural and unassuming seaside community

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Spanish architect Jaime and his wife Mimi as they embark on a mission to convert a Grade II listed folly into a family home in Buckinghamshire. Giving themselves a wildly optimistic six-month deadline to finish before the birth of their second child, it's not long before the couple hit trouble. Jaime puts all his energies and creativity into the project, using innovative 3D mapping to squeeze living spaces into the tower, but it proves to be an exhausting struggle

programme.title: Little British Isles with Alison Steadman
programme.desc: The actress Alison Steadman celebrates the natural beauty of some of the 6,000 islands that surround mainland Britain. Inspired by memories of a childhood holiday on the Isle of Man, Alison sets out to meet some of the people who live on the UK's 267 inhabited islands. She begins on Scotland's windswept Inner Hebrides, exploring the sparsely populated Gigha, Jura, Colonsay and Oronsay over the course of a week. She learns how to extract orchid essence, visits a fish farm, meets the keeper in charge of the country's only native honey bee reserve, and meets the resourceful natives thriving off the tough land that sustains them

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Headteacher Emma arrives in resus having been in a collision with a bus while cycling home from the school she works at. As she is treated, her husband Mick reflects on being informed what had happened. Meanwhile, 33-year-old Josh is treated for severe back pain, and 87-year-old Pamela is examined for a possible blood clot in her leg, and tells the doctors about her childhood in London during the Blitz

programme.title: Sex Party Secrets
programme.desc: As the popularity of exclusive arranged orgies rises, this documentary explores the hidden world of the UK's self-proclaimed 'sexual elite', providing an insight into the lives of the most selective party planners and their meticulously vetted clients. As the guests sip champagne and fulfil their wildest fantasies behind the doors of multimillion-pound mansions and country estates, the programme explores the motivation behind the desire that drives married couples, young single friends and seasoned sex addicts to seek fleeting intimate experiences and group encounters with strangers

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy version of the words-and-numbers quiz, with Sean Lock and Stephen Mangan teaming up against Noel Fielding and Fay Ripley. The Brett Domino Trio are in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Headteacher Emma arrives in resus having been in a collision with a bus while cycling home from the school she works at. As she is treated, her husband Mick reflects on being informed what had happened. Meanwhile, 33-year-old Josh is treated for severe back pain, and 87-year-old Pamela is examined for a possible blood clot in her leg, and tells the doctors about her childhood in London during the Blitz

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick treats a border collie in a lot of pain with a suspicious lump on its ankle. However, the dog's recovery does not go to plan and proves extremely challenging. A short-haired cat arrives with a sore hip from jumping off the sofa, and it seems a hip replacement may be the best option, and a golden labrador is rushed into Fitzpatrick's sister clinic in Guildford with a lump growing on its side

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt investigates concerns about levels of naturally occurring arsenic in baby rice products, Jimmy Doherty examines Marks & Spencer's claim that they can trace all their beefburgers back to the cow, and Kate Quilton find out whether a parasite could be hiding in sushi. Programme exploring the secrets behind mass-produced food, meeting technicians, scientists, factory owners, growers and producers to find out exactly what goes into the groceries that end up on the supermarket shelves

programme.desc: Mike and Frank follow a trail of junk in Illinois and find themselves waist-deep in a giant pile of bizarre items

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Lori and Brandon win one of two aviation storage units that have come up for sale, while biker Dan bags the other

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Brandon and Lori find a hot-air balloon in their newly acquired trailer

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank pick through a millionaire's collection and find a valuable rare car part. Danielle gets a chance to join the guys on their travels

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank look for bargains in the collection of a former pawn shop owner, and receive good news about one of their purchases from a museum

programme.title: Cop Car Workshop
programme.desc: John and Dave get rid of unwanted skips and trailers, which are home to some unusual objects. Elsewhere, emergency repairs are required to get two police bikes back out on the road

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May revisit their bid to build amphibious vehicles, and face one of their toughest challenges yet. The Audi R8 takes to the track and Jools Holland tests his driving ability as the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gets to grips with a Peel P50, the smallest car to go into production, and finds a surprising number of uses for it. James May experiences the luxury of the Rolls-Royce Phantom Drophead Coupe convertible, and the Stig puts the Ferrari 599 GTB Fiorano through its paces on the test track. The Rolling Stones' Ronnie Wood is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Rick Stein's Far Eastern Odyssey
programme.desc: The chef continues his exploration of the Far East's most diverse food cultures, travelling to Penang, Malaysia, to sample its multifaceted cuisine, which includes Malay, Indian, Chinese and British flavours. He also meets food writer Fay Khoo to explore the popularity of the region's fish-head curry, before crossing the Indian Ocean to Sri Lanka, where he visits a bustling fishing village and takes part in an unusual expedition

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May find the fastest way to get around London - bike, boat or car - in a race through the capital during rush hour. The Stig takes to the test track in the Caparo T1, the Aston Martin V8 Vantage Roadster is pitted against a man with three rockets strapped to his back, and Simon Cowell is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson test-drives two estate cars - the BMW M5 and Mercedes E63. Richard Hammond tries out motor-home racing, while James May pits an Alfa Romeo 159 against a man who crosses the Humber estuary without using its famous bridge. The Stig puts Honda's Civic Type R through its paces and former England rugby union forward Lawrence Dallaglio is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Eddie Eats America
programme.desc: Eddie gets a taste of Cuba in Miami's fresh seafood, sets his sights at a shooting range, and heads cross-state to attempt a huge surf & turf challenge created just for him

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Reluctant tourist Richard Ayoade is joined by comedian Matt Lucas for a frenetic 48 hours in the Italian capital. Their first challenge is finding suitable headgear to protect from the scorching Italian sun. They then take a guided tour in vintage Fiat 500 cars, and after wending their way past the Circus Maximus finish atop Gianicolo Hill in time for the cannon being fired - a daily ritual since the 1850s. Next they head to the border of the Vatican, before experiencing the very best of modern Italian cuisine. Day two sees visits to the Trevi Fountain and the Colosseum, perhaps Rome's most incredible antiquity

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by comedian Sara Pascoe for a two-day test of the mini break merits of Valencia, Spain's third largest city. After dropping their bags at the Hotel Caro, they set off along the path of the Turia river. Richard also enrols them at paella school to make authentic and vegan versions of the dish. The next day begins with a visit to Valencia's iconic City of Arts and Sciences, home to an Imax cinema, planetarium, opera house and aquarium, though Richard and Sara forgo these attractions to try zorbing instead

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May compete in a 1950s-style race from London to Edinburgh. On the footplate of the A1 Tornado steam train, Jeremy has to feed the boiler with a constant supply of coal, while Richard braves the saddle of a Vincent Black Shadow motorbike and James drives an old Jaguar XK120 as the trio discover what Top Gear might have been like 60 years ago. Michael Schumacher drives the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade and comedian Joe Lycett head to the mini break mecca of Amsterdam. Their two-day hurtle through the city's attractions takes in culture, canals, cuisine, and other things not necessarily beginning with C. As part of their whistle-stop tour, the duo doss down in one of the city's hippest hotels, take a battery-powered boat tour, do some drawing, and peer into the world of the water bear. The brave travellers also sample some Dutch gin known as jenever, and visit the city's street organ museum

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade and actress Sally Phillips head to the Swedish capital for a fulsome two days of fun. Despite having no head for heights, the duo fearlessly sample the city's only rooftop tour, before wrestling with the concept of fika - coffee and cake. The pair also a visit to the museum established in homage to 1970s pop group Abba and attend an interactive late-night art show involving their phones. After all this, there's still time to sample some meatballs, ride on a pedalo and experience the world's first ice bar

programme.title: Monster Ships
programme.desc: The work carried out by ONE Columba, which is capable of carrying 14,000 shipping containers, but has the dangerous challenge of navigating twisting rivers and ancient ports

programme.title: Monster Ships
programme.desc: Battling harsh conditions, we follow Algoma Sault on a 2000km odyssey across the Great Lakes

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson joins an unprecedented archaeological expedition to Belgium in search of a perfectly preserved WWI bunker called the Vampire.

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates this documentary chronicling events leading up to the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbour on December 7, 1941, and the subsequent collapse of unprepared British forces before the advancing enemy in Singapore. Includes interviews with veterans from both sides

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The story of Hitler's personal secretary Martin Bormann and the efforts to track him down. With insufficient evidence to confirm the Nazi official's death, the International Military Tribunal at Nuremberg tried him in absentia during 1946 and eventually sentenced him to death. Despite extensive efforts, he was never found and was eventually presumed to have died in May 1945

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The underground base with the power to destroy a nation, the site linked with an attempt to kill a vicious dictator, and an ancient world discovered in the open American wilderness

programme.title: Moon Landings: World's Greatest Hoax?
programme.desc: Examining the most famous conspiracy theories behind the 1969 Apollo space program that saw Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin walk on the moon - or did they?

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Kirby Hall in Northamptonshire, where members of the public bring along treasured antiques and collectibles to hear their stories and find out their worth. Items include a jade bowl once used as a butter dish, marine paintings from the family of Captain Bligh, a vintage TV set with a four-inch screen, a medieval ring found with the aid of a metal detector and a wind-up Charlie Chaplin doll. In the new feature looking back at previous items, art specialist Philip Mould hopes to learn more about a 500-year-old religious painting, originally featured in 2010 and now fully restored

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: The small, picturesque seaside station at Dawlish in Devon is brought to life by an influx of people attending an air show, and the company lays on extra staff, schedules more trains and employs different queuing systems to cope with all the passengers. At Bath Spa, gate-line operator Peter Eagle tries his hand at foreign languages as he directs international tourists to and from the ancient city

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Maritime engineering in recent years has seen ever bigger ships and sea-based platforms constructed. On occasion, they may need moving, which is the purpose of the Boka Vanguard, the planet's largest heavy lift ship, capable of sinking itself, floating under its cargo and then lifting it up to be moved anywhere on the planet with sea. This extraordinary work of engineering is capable of carrying the Eiffel Tower and the Statue of Liberty at the same time

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Claustrophobic prison cells that contain a story worthy of international acclaim, and a vast mountainside complex that held the power to change the world

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: How the SAS War Crimes Investigation Team tried to pinpoint the location of Gestapo officers who were wanted for a host of atrocities

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Factors influencing America's decision to support Britain during the Second World War, and how Hitler's declaration of war on the US convinced Congress to take more decisive action. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo uses his 1913 copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide as he travels through Spain and Portugal. He begins in the city of La Coruna, where he examines the Celtic roots of the Galician people and tries to master the bagpipes, before meeting walkers from all over the world on the pilgrims' trail to Santiago de Compostela. Heading across the border to Porto, Michael finds out about the origins of Britain's long alliance with the Portuguese, and finishes his journey in the capital Lisbon

programme.title: Inside Central Station
programme.desc: A special festive peek behind the scenes at Glasgow Central. The tree is up, the Christmas shoppers are out and there is a very special visitor to the station concourse

programme.title: Steam Train Britain
programme.desc: On the Welsh Highland Railway, a newly built Pullman carriage is on its way to Caernarfon Station and a Saddle Tank Loco is being completely restored on the East Lancashire Railway

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn visits Swindon's railway village, which became home to Great Western's enormous works and he also explores the dark and secret past of Milan's station

programme.title: The Two Ronnies

programme.title: Monster Ships
programme.desc: The work carried out by ONE Columba, which is capable of carrying 14,000 shipping containers, but has the dangerous challenge of navigating twisting rivers and ancient ports

programme.title: Time Team

He begins in the city of La Coruna, where he examines the Celtic roots of the Galician people and tries to master the bagpipes, before meeting walkers from all over the world on the pilgrims' trail to Santiago de Compostela. Heading across the border to Porto, Michael finds out about the origins of Britain's long alliance with the Portuguese, and finishes his journey in the capital Lisbon

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team pay a return visit to Kirby Hall in Northamptonshire, where items of interest include Victorian jewellery, Art Deco enamel, a toy football team, possibly the world's smallest stuffed dog and a Venetian drug jar that might be worth a fortune - but only if it's original. Expert Paul Atterbury also casts his eye over an elaborate carved chair - inspired by, of all things, the MPs' expenses scandal

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: Bruce Parkin steps off a train with his family to attend his retirement party after 52 years' service, making him one of Europe's longest-serving drivers. The redevelopment of Reading station is the largest overhaul on the Great Western network since the days of Brunel, and Murray Parkin and his team carry out the last engine change at the historic Cow Lane site before they head over to their new state-of-the-art terminal

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: How in devising the F35 Lightnin II, engineers have pushed the boundaries of aviation technology further than ever before and created a brand new, revolutionary, fighter aircraft capable of handling threats in the air and on the ground. Plus, Rob Collings flies one of the most successful aircraft of the Second World War, the distinctive P38, former RAF test pilot Andy Edgell tracks down the product of a top secret research project, the X-14 and retired US Air Force pilot Mace Carpenter reconnects with the stealthy F117

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A secretive underground base that concealed the power to destroy nations, and a sprawling industrial relic clinging to the edge of an unforgiving waterfall

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The story of the Allied forces hunt for Joachim Peiper, the man responsible for the massacre of American PoWs in Malmedy, Belgium

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The battle for North Africa, which involved three years of fighting between Rommel's Afrika Korps and Montgomery's Desert Rats and culminated in the Allied victory at El Alamein. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo uses his 1913 copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide to venture beyond Europe as he travels through the Holy Land. He begins in Israel, learning about the Baha'i faith and how it spread to Britain in the Edwardian era, hearing the story of the origins of Tel Aviv, and visiting the Christian, Jewish and Muslim quarters of the capital Jerusalem. Michael then crosses the separation barrier between Jerusalem and the West Bank in the company of his Palestinian guide and meets the embroiderers of the Arab Women's Union in Bethlehem, before finishing his journey in the Negev desert

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A small isolated country littered with strange concrete mushrooms and a secret Japanese facility that was once at the forefront of weapons technology

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Scone Palace in Perthshire, which they first visited back in March, discovering several items with historical connections. They include a 19th-century military campaign box said to have been stolen from Napoleon, a portrait of a woman who helped save the lives of hundreds of Jews in occupied Holland during the Second World War and a silver tea service that was once to be melted down to raise funds for a Spitfire. A teacher also brings in a leather strap, a fearsome implement of corporal punishment that wasn't outlawed until the 1980s

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: A major service disruption hits Paddington on Meet the Manager day, and there is a look at Brunel's last engineering masterpiece, the Royal Albert Bridge, which spans the River Tamar and connects Devon and Cornwall. A fitter has to repair a faulty peak-time train full of passengers, and Charlbury station prepares for the Wilderness festival

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: New York Architect Wendy Fok travels to the remote Guizhou Valley in China to see the Beipanjiang First Bridge, the highest bridge on Earth, which boasts a 20,000 tonne steel deck. She soon discovers that trying to straddle a 565 metre gorge so deep that it's known as the 'crack in the Earth', presented huge challenges for its engineers. Plus, Andrew Steele tracks down a bridge on the thames that nobody thought could ever be built, Suzie Sheehy is in Hampshire climbing the tallest unreinforced concrete town ever built and Luke Bisby scales the Forth Bridge in Edinburgh

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A marooned ironclad structure off the coast of England that hides a dramatic story of war and resilience, and a facility in the heart of America that helped tear apart a nation

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The pursuit of former Austrian policeman Franz Stangl, who served as commandant of the Nazi extermination camps Sobibor and Treblinka

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How the myth of German invincibility was finally debunked by their defeat at the Battle of Stalingrad. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo follows in the footsteps of Edwardian travellers to trace a route recommended in his Bradshaw's guide from the heart of France to the Mediterranean coast. His journey begins in Lyon, where he learns how the city got its gastronomic reputation, and takes instruction from a leading chef on making an omelette. In Avignon, he samples a glass of the region's famous wine Chateauneuf-du-Pape, before his journey ends at the Mediterranean port of Marseille, where he joins a pilot boat as it leads a supertanker to its berth

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra wonders how Ray would cope if she met an untimely end, and picks an attractive friend to replace her as his new wife - but cannot help being hurt by his enthusiastic response to the idea

programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Arvada, Colorado, where a husband and wife have allowed their restaurant to fall into disrepair. programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits PJ's Steakhouse in Queens, New York, to provide his expert advice to the owners. programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.desc: A look back at previous first dates that did not go smoothly, including make-up artist Demi's awkward meeting with 29-year-old Danny, and retired NHS counsellor Elaine's date with music producer Steve, which appeared to be going well until it came to settling the bill. Plus, a look back at Disney superfans James and Jess's date

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: Two years ago, George meets Armin and Amifa, whose dark and dingy 1920s house in Bellingham, south east London, was not just ugly - the DIY extension added by former owners had been condemned and the makeshift kitchen was an accident waiting to happen. Architect Carl Turner did what he could with the £80,000 budget, but in the end the upstairs was left unfinished. George now returns to see how Armin and Amifa have tackled the top floor

programme.title: Is Covid Racist?
programme.desc: A&E medic Dr Ronx sets out to understand why so many NHS colleagues who died from coronavirus came from Black, Asian and minority ethnic groups. Featuring stories of frontline workers who have lost their lives, and a striking visual tribute in memory of many more, Dr Ronx asks what is being done to protect frontline BAME workers

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Chartered surveyor Mike Aldous hosts the first dinner party in Surrey, beginning by visiting an off-licence to choose a wine to accompany each course. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Businesswoman Amanda Keeling throws the second party in Surrey, raising the stakes by hosting the event at her palatial home, and employing the services of a butler. However, the plan backfires when her guests suggest she cheated by hiring an extra pair of hands, and things go from bad to worse when Mike and Terry come to blows

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: School cookery club teacher Maggie Thomson hosts the third dinner party challenge in Surrey, and prepares a menu that includes boeuf bourguignon and cheese tart. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mother-of-five Rachel Underwood hosts the penultimate dinner party in Surrey, taking a relaxed approach with tried-and-tested recipes. However, rival Mike Aldous claims her menu is far too easy and her choice of pudding ends in disaster

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fitness fanatic Terry Gallagher opens his home for the final dinner party in Surrey, hoping to win the £1,000 prize by serving a healthy low-fat menu after four high-calorie meals earlier in the week. programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Chris and Kay from Buckinghamshire are searching for their perfect retirement home to share with their two cats. The only hitch is that they can't agree on where it should be. Kay loves the stunning scenery of Cardigan Bay in west Wales, while sun-worshipper Chris would rather move to the beautiful Mar Menor in Spain. Jasmine Harman selects three properties for their £150,000 budget in Wales, but Jonnie Irwin has three cracking options in the Mar Menor

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Robert asks if he can borrow the suitcase sitting on the stairway landing, Ray explains he and Debra have been locked in a two-week battle of wills over who is responsible for putting the item away. Ray Romano and Brad Garrett star

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra and Marie shame Ray into giving Robert a second bachelor party to make up for his disappointing first attempt

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Florida to help the owners of Fleming, a Danish restaurant in Miami. programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Dance Around the World

programme.title: Surviving the Stone Age

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Swedish-American Judi Lembke hosts the first dinner party in London, hoping her relaxed attitude and an aperitif of schnapps will put the guests at ease and help her win the £1,000 cash prize. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Grace reacts badly to revelations about her new boyfriend, while Will and Jack discover they are both dating the same man. Comedy, starring Eric McCormack and Debra Messing

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Karen tries to help caffeine-addicted Jack kick the habit, but ends up fighting cravings of her own. Will is annoyed that Grace appears to be acting more like a wife than an old friend during Joe and Larry's civil partnership ceremony

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan starts sleepwalking, and Charlie feels duty-bound to cure him. Meanwhile, Jake starts seeing a therapist. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen, Jon Cryer and Angus T Jones

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Jake's inappropriate remarks at a meeting of Judith's divorcee support group prompt his furious mother to tell Alan she does not want her son living with Charlie any longer. The womanising musician goes to the next meeting and struggles to convince the ladies he has a good influence on his nephew

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Updates on the story of property developer Jon Morgan, who called in an eviction specialist when the disused tram station he bought as an investment was taken over by squatters. Plus, a couple who rented out their home while they moved to America - only for their tenant to end up owing them £10,000 in back payments - and how a team from Peterborough Council came across a house with life-threatening electrics and some of the worst damp and mould they had ever seen

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: People featured in previous episodes, including an Essex landlady who wondered why her tenant had stopped paying rent - only to discover that the woman was in prison for GBH and that her ex-boyfriend had moved into the flat. Plus, a music entrepreneur who took his tenant to court after receiving no rent for seven months

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Matt and Garry are in Wolverhampton, where a woman who owes £2,000 in parking fines denies knowing the debtor - despite being a relative. But when her mother also gets involved, tempers start to fray. Stewart and Iain confront a woman in Cheshire who is refusing to pay fees to a veterinary practice, claiming her dog has been misdiagnosed, while Paul and Max collect £5,000 owed by a banned company director in east London accused of mis-selling assets on the stock market

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Gary and Paul visit Kent to recover £2,500 owed by a publican to a utilities supplier - only to find themselves in the middle of a family at war. Stewart and Elmor deal with a sensitive case in Liverpool, as they repossess a property where the tenant has failed to pay any rent for almost a year

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Documentary series giving an insight into the world of debt collection from the perspectives of debtors, creditors and those who collect the payments. Brian and Graham have been warned to expect trouble as they evict some tenants from a south London flat, one of whom has a reputation for violence. Everything goes smoothly until the enforcement agents make a shocking discovery in a child's bedroom. Paul and Steve head to Slough to collect wages owed to a freelance worker, but the debtor is less than happy to find the high-court enforcement officers on his doorstep and things get physical

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Sheriff Paul Bohill tackles the case of a small debt owed by ex-footballer Neil 'Razor' Ruddock to a kennel owner, before being forced to evict a school dinner lady in south London. Meanwhile, in the north of England enforcement agent Stewart MacCracken heads out with trainee Iain Taylor to confront a Yorkshire builder they need to get payment from at his home, while a reptile shop owner hit by online competitors urgently needs to pay a debt for the feed company he relies on to keep his animals alive

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A drug dealer who has been driving dangerously reveals a surprising amount of information when he is pulled over, and friends try to baffle the pursuit team by changing their names. Meanwhile, an officer has a shock in store for a law-breaking driver

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: An officer is nearly run over by a desperate driver, and another's patience is tested by a drunken motorist. Plus, a law enforcer goes in search of a suspicious character, and a group of youths tries to deceive the interception unit

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: It is Halloween and Jane spots Dipi kissing a costumed man she believes to be Shane, while Harlow sets aside her anger when she realises that Hendrix might be leaving

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. The programme reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed

programme.title: Britain's Parking Hell
programme.desc: Police in Hereford make anti-social pavement parking a top priority, but their operation results in emergency services finding themselves stuck in the middle. Meanwhile, At Weston-super-Mare, it's one of the busiest days of the year—the beach race day, which proves difficult for traffic enforcement officers to control

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Horse-rescue boss Jill rushes to the aid of an animal abandoned at a roadside. Young couple Nicole and Aaron plan to buy and refurbish a campervan so they can travel the world putting on fitness boot camps. Former call-centre employee Courtney and her boyfriend Michael get the chance to run their own bar, although their boss is soon piling on the pressure to get it open as soon as possible

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Following the hidden army of enforcement teams and inspectors out to nail the litter louts and the fearless sewage warriors clearing the mess

programme.title: Extreme Fare Dodgers
programme.desc: With special access to Transport for London's revenue protection teams, this documentary tells the stories of frontline enforcement officers and undercover investigators fighting back against passengers dodging fares across the English capital ever day

programme.title: The Clues That Caught the Killer - Sadie Hartley
programme.desc: Documentary examining the vital breakthroughs that helped police catch the killers of 60-year-old Sadie Hartley, who was immobilised with a stun gun and stabbed to death in her own home in January 2016. Detectives quickly identified two suspects, but only patient and thorough police work could uncover the evidence to secure a conviction

programme.title: The Lesbian Guide to Straight Sex
programme.desc: A London couple who feel their strict Muslim upbringing left them in the dark in the bedrtoom department get tips on how to improve their sex life

programme.title: Wentworth Prison
programme.desc: New arrival Judy Bryant is welcomed into the Wentworth prison family with open arms when she is instrumental in forcing prison management to change the food supplier

programme.desc: Ann announces the closure of the Protection Unit, forcing Will and Vera into preservation mode, while Allie refuses to guarantee Marie's safety, leaving the prison on a knife edge

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie hurts Alan's feelings by asking him to stay out of the house while he has friends over, so Rose steps in to cheer him up - but finds his gloom hard to dispel. Sean Penn, Elvis Costello and Harry Dean Stanton guest star

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Paul discovers that an unruly tenant may be planning to do a runner, Harrow Council discovers 30 people in a three-bedroom house, and shopowner Pauline is threatened when squatters invade her property

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Letting agent Paul has a tenant in Lancashire who is behind with his rent and won't return any calls, but the situation is much worse than he could possibly have imagined when he heads into the upstairs bedroom. Harrow enforcement officers Andrew and Ozge are called to a local salon, where a leak from the upstairs flat is disrupting Masi's ability to run her shop. Once the landlord agrees to give them access, they discover terrible conditions and a back yard piled with rubbish

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Documentary series giving an insight into the world of debt collection from the perspectives of debtors, creditors and those who collect the payments. Brian and Graham have been warned to expect trouble as they evict some tenants from a south London flat, one of whom has a reputation for violence. Everything goes smoothly until the enforcement agents make a shocking discovery in a child's bedroom. Paul and Steve head to Slough to collect wages owed to a freelance worker, but the debtor is less than happy to find the high-court enforcement officers on his doorstep and things get physical

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Sheriff Paul Bohill tackles the case of a small debt owed by ex-footballer Neil 'Razor' Ruddock to a kennel owner, before being forced to evict a school dinner lady in south London. Meanwhile, in the north of England enforcement agent Stewart MacCracken heads out with trainee Iain Taylor to confront a Yorkshire builder they need to get payment from at his home, while a reptile shop owner hit by online competitors urgently needs to pay a debt for the feed company he relies on to keep his animals alive

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Enforcement officers are faced with the difficult job of evicting a woman with two children - a task that takes a turn for the worse when her ex-partner makes an appearance. In Liverpool, locals threaten a riot over an unpaid debt at a computer shop, while two agents come across an extremely rare car in the course of their duties

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Steve discover a locked wooden case when they are called to evict tenants from a flat in London's Docklands. Opening it up, they realise they urgently need police back-up - and the property quickly becomes a crime scene. Brian and Graham head to Swindon Town FC to collect a debt, while Paul's team arrives at a property in Essex to find that they have to remove a woman and her six children

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Two known offenders end up in trouble again after a high-speed chase. An encounter with a gang of hoodies leads to a close shave for one officer, another goes in pursuit of a four-legged fugitive, and a Lamborghini turns out to be not all it seems

programme.title: Police Interceptors

After tracking down the landlord, they have mercy and allow him to spend his £5,000 fine on fixing up the property, in the hope he will turn it into a nice family home. But will the he be as good as his word?

programme.desc: Conclusion of a three-part story. The investigation into a movie studio boss's murder becomes further complicated when fresh evidence points to yet another suspect. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A woman is attacked and killed just hours after a serial rapist is freed on parole, prompting McCoy to pull out all the stops to bring him to justice - but before long the detective realises the wanted man could be innocent. Drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.desc: Petty officer Kyle Friedgen is accused of killing his ex-girlfriend, but while his case is dismissed due to a mishandled warrant, his life and reputation are marred by suspicion that he got away with murder. Determined to clear his name in full, Friedgen volunteers to be court-martialed, on the condition that the NCIS team conducts its own investigation. Meanwhile, Bishop and Jake discuss their marital problems. Naval crime drama, starring Emily Wickersham, with Jamie Bamber

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The two Marine brothers of a sailor battling leukaemia are killed in the line of duty, and to spare the servicemen's family further grief, the secretary of the US Navy seeks the NCIS team's help in locating a suitable bone marrow donor. Meanwhile, Bishop returns home to Oklahoma for Thanksgiving. Military crime drama, starring Emily Wickersham

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The first instalment of a two-part crossover with NCIS: New Orleans sees the Washington, DC team co-operating with their colleagues from the Big Easy to investigate a spate of lethal poisonings on board a New Orleans-to-DC flight. All the evidence seems to point to missing chef Luca, who happens to be Abby's brother, so the two investigative services join forces to locate him, and find out why a private tech firm seems to have been targetted by the killer. Naval crime drama, guest starring Scott Bakula

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team suspects foul play when a seaman who was set to testify before a grand jury dies before he can make his court appearance. When it becomes clear the man was murdered, the subsequent investigation reveals that a previously thwarted human-trafficking ring has been re-established. Meanwhile, a storm causes a massive power outage, and McGee seeks a room for the night among his colleagues. Naval crime drama, starring Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A deep-sea diver is murdered on the job, and his co-workers all become suspects. However, when they return to the surface, they must remain in a high-tech decompression chamber for four days. With each of their suspects confined within a tank, the NCIS team must complete an unorthodox investigation from the outside. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.desc: Briscoe and Curtis investigate the murder of a model and discover splashes of blood that obviously did not come from the victim, but suggest the culprit is a relative. With Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Curtis discover a man leading a secret double life when they investigate who opened fire on a group of people disembarking from a party cruise. A major dispute erupts when Schiff confronts the governor and attorney general over the decision to seek the death penalty. Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach star

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A pizza delivery man is murdered, causing trouble when the two suspects point the finger at the other. Though one of the pair is taped admitting to the crime, his Catholic priest uncle forbids the team from violating the sanctity of the Confessional. Drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Sarah Porter, the Secretary of the Navy, learns her daughter has been kidnapped, and the NCIS crew teams up with the FBI to track the case, determine a motive and bring the abducted girl home. Meanwhile, McGee's childhood friend, NCIS Special Agent Valerie Page, arrives in town to assist with advanced tactics training. Naval crime drama, guest starring Leslie Hope and Christina Chang

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team looks into a raid on a naval base, and Dr Cyril Taft - the surgeon who saved Gibbs' life - experiences the thrill of assisting on an investigation. Assigned to treat Gibbs' suspect, Taft manages to uncover evidence that may prove crucial to solving the case. Meanwhile, DiNozzo pays his old flame Jeanne Benoit a visit as he chases a lead involving doctors in Sudan. Guest starring John Cryer and Scottie Thompson

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A day spent working on a cut-and-dried case leaves the team free to spend some time away from work, and while McGee and Delilah argue over the importance of keeping shop-talk to a minimum, DiNozzo ends up boring his dinner date with crime-solving tales. However, the agents' personal plans are all interrupted as, one-by-one, they realise errors have been made in closing their latest investigation. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An American couple are attacked in Iraq, in the very same spot a group of US Special Ops snipers were ambushed six months earlier. Determined to get to the bottom of the case, Gibbs is forced to speak to Aaron Davis, the only survivor of the first assault, who is now struggling through PTSD at the Walter Reed Medical Centre. The 300th episode of the military crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren and Eames investigate when the owner of one of the city's most exclusive hotels is found dead from a fatal injection of Botox, and suspicion falls on her unstable daughter, who resented her mother's interference in her love life. Crime drama, starring Vincent D'Onofrio and Kathryn Erbe

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: When an infant goes missing, detectives take sides in the family's right-to-die court case and Barba's interference in the case puts the whole DA's office in jeopardy. Drama, starring Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A doctor, arrested for sexually abusing his patients, is set free after Cassidy's testimony causes a mistrial. Lorenzo Scott and Dean Winters guest star

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A computer genius is accused of murdering his girlfriend, but responds to the team's investigation by threatening to expose their darkest secrets

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Barba ask witnesses to risk their jobs when a news anchor makes a rape accusation against her boss on the air

programme.title: Criminal Minds: Beyond Borders
programme.desc: The body of an American singer is found on a rural road in the Blue Mountains of Jamaica, prompting Garrett and his International Response Team to investigate

programme.title: Criminal Minds: Beyond Borders
programme.desc: Two Americans are murdered while on a yoga retreat in Nepal, and when the team investigates, it becomes clear the locals believe the killings were the work of the mythological yeti

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. The programme reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed

programme.title: Ghost Whisperer

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: Morgana begins to wonder whether she has magic powers, but Gaius insists she remain ignorant of her potential for her own good. Merlin refuses to accept the decision, and in wilfully disobeying his master, he reunites Morgana with Mordred, the boy the Dragon predicts will one day kill Arthur. Drama, starring Katie McGrath, Richard Wilson and Colin Morgan, with Colin Salmon

programme.title: Fred & Rose: The Unanswered Questions
programme.desc: Twenty-five years after the police searched the Gloucester home of Fred and Rose West, this documentary asks how many more girls the couple might have killed

programme.title: Countdown to Murder
programme.desc: The events leading up to the killing of landscape architect Joanna Yeates by Vincent Tabak in Bristol on the night of December 17, 2010. Featuring dramatic reconstructions and interviews with Joanna's parents David and Teresa Yeates, her landlord Christopher Jeffries and CPS prosecutor Ann Reddrop

programme.title: Brit Cops

programme.title: The Force: Essex

programme.title: Banged Up Abroad

programme.title: UK Border Force
programme.desc: Documentary series taking viewers inside the UK Border Agency, revealing the work that takes place behind the scenes of the immigration process. Teams at Heathrow Terminal 3, Calais, Dover and across the North West and North East of England reveal the methods and technology used to allow legitimate visitors safe passage while catching those who try to gain illegal entry to the UK

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The zebra plans a surprise for Bertie and McMeer

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The zebras watch shooting stars and McMeer decides he wants to have one of his own

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: The phoenixes are migrating, so all the villagers are desperate to get their hands on a magic fiery feather to keep them warm through the winter

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: Jack and Tashi are sucked into the Book of Tales, where they encounter a mixed-up monster and a knight who has lost his sword and shield

programme.title: Nils Holgersson
programme.desc: Re-joining the flock, Nils and Martin are caught by a group of crows who have come to join the Black Elf

programme.title: Nils Holgersson
programme.desc: Smirre repeatedly attacks the geese, who only just manage to escape

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: Norwich couple Simon and Caitlin embark on an ambitious project under the watchful eye of expert Sarah Beeny. They plan to buy a former council house, invest all their savings and completely renovate it in the hope that a subsequent sale will raise enough money to move onto the next rung of the property ladder

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb presents the best properties she found on her real-estate tour of Florida and the Caribbean in the show that aims to advise British househunters looking for a second home abroad

programme.title: Wildlife
programme.desc: Following the fate of the 'Dunlop' family of foxes in Essex as they fight for survival in the wild. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: How southerners reacted to the threat of Hitler's V1 flying bombs, which killed 452 people in a week at one point in the campaign. Notoriously inaccurate, the doodlebugs often fell short of their target and hit villages along the south coast from Kent to Dorset, prompting thousands in Chiselhurst to start living in caves

programme.title: The Bionic Vet
programme.desc: A woman with a seven-month-old puppy faces a difficult decision when Noel Fitzpatrick reveals he must perform a risky operation to treat the young dog's bone deformities. Two animals injured in road accidents are rushed into the surgery and a Labrador puts its newly transplanted hip to the test when it is reunited with its owner

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing Brockley, Peckham and Rotherhithe. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Where Angels Fear to Tread (1991)
programme.desc: A wealthy widow travels to Italy to escape the pressure of her in-laws, and promptly falls in love with a poor local. When the revived woman agrees to marry her foreign friend, her relatives back home are shocked by the apparent disregard for class, culture and their family's image, and set about putting an end to the unlikely union. Period drama, based on the novel by EM Forster, starring Helena Bonham Carter, Judy Davis and Rupert Graves

programme.title: One Good Turn (1954)
programme.desc: A boy living in an orphanage grows up to be the institution's lovable but clumsy caretaker. He must save the children's home from a wicked property developer who wants to build a factory on the site, and tricks him into taking the kids to the seaside so he can knock the building down. Comedy, starring Norman Wisdom and Joan Rice

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Fay Davies advises a couple from Cornwall looking for a home in the western part of Portugal's picturesque Algarve. They are hoping their £100,000 budget will buy them a property set in the area's rolling landscapes and beautiful beaches

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett selects memorable moments from the house-hunting series as she returns to some of her favourite locations. From Woolacombe to Whitby, and Margate to the Isle of Wight, the retrospective illustrates why the English coastline has become the latest property hotspot

programme.title: D-Day: The Shortest Day
programme.desc: Survivors provide a compelling insight into the D-Day landings, discussing the preparations, their actions, and the consequences of an operation which had such a crucial bearing on the outcome of the Second World War

programme.title: Once a Soldier
programme.desc: Open for over 300 years and home to 320 old soldiers, the Royal Hospital in Chelsea is under threat. This series follows the residents for a year of their lives

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: How southerners reacted to the threat of Hitler's V1 flying bombs, which killed 452 people in a week at one point in the campaign. Notoriously inaccurate, the doodlebugs often fell short of their target and hit villages along the south coast from Kent to Dorset, prompting thousands in Chiselhurst to start living in caves

programme.title: Murder Investigation Team
programme.desc: The team are called to a lake where the body of a vagrant has been found - and within minutes discover a second body, that of a teacher, has been pushed from his fifth-floor window. Despite the very different circumstances, the investigators still suspect there's a link. Lindsey Coulson and Samantha Spiro star in the gritty police procedural drama, with a guest appearance by John Duttine

programme.title: IBA Boxing
programme.desc: IBA Boxing brings you the best of the adrenaline pumping fights and knock out action from their legendary events.

programme.title: My Saviour (2019)
programme.desc: A woman whose life is in disarray is attacked by a local gang, only for a mysterious stranger to come to her rescue. She forms an intense relationship with her rescuer, but their close bond sends her on a disturbing journey into a nightmarish world of violence and abuse. Drama, starring Bayley Freer and Steven Murphy

programme.title: Hot Property (2016)
programme.desc: A corporate spy loses her job after getting caught embezzling, and is no longer able to fund her lavish lifestyle. Faced with being evicted from her rented home, she puts all her skills of deception into fending off the landlord, replacement tenants and a psychotic estate agent. Comedy, starring MyAnna Buring and Tom Rhys Harries

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb advises British house-hunters looking for a place in Crete, showing them round a traditional Greek home, a modern village property and an old Venetian-style farm, each for less than £60,000

programme.title: Operation Third Form (1966)
programme.desc: A trio of youngsters tries to stop a gang of crooks and save an old junk collector from being accused of theft. Children's drama, starring Kevin Bennett and Sydney Bromley

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: A south London couple gamble their savings in an attempt to raise enough cash to buy their dream home in the countryside. The plan is to renovate their two-bedroom former council flat so they can make money by renting it out for as much as possible. Property developer Sarah Beeny keeps an eye on their progress

programme.title: D-Day: The Shortest Day
programme.desc: Survivors provide a compelling insight into the D-Day landings, discussing the preparations, their actions, and the consequences of an operation which had such a crucial bearing on the outcome of the Second World War

programme.title: Once a Soldier
programme.desc: Open for over 300 years and home to 320 old soldiers, the Royal Hospital in Chelsea is under threat. This series follows the residents for a year of their lives

programme.desc: Hitler's second Vengeance weapon, the deadly V2 rocket, which was responsible for killing 3,000 people in London and the south of England during six terrifying months

programme.title: The Bionic Vet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick prepares to amputate a corgi's cancerous paw and replace it with a prosthetic foot, a procedure that has only been carried out on two other dogs. He also catches up with a puppy that was suffering from a painful bone deformity, and treats a barn owl brought into the surgery with a broken wing

programme.title: London Districts

programme.desc: A British army officer searches for a Jewish refugee in post-war Germany, only to discover that she has lost her memory. Romantic mystery, starring Robert Beatty and Mai Zetterling

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A report on the case of Jake and Victoria Ward, who were investigated by police and social services when they were unable to explain a serious injury to their three-month-old son. It took the couple two years to clear their names and a further three to win the right to speak openly about what happened to them. Darragh MacIntyre explores their fight to open up the closed world of the family courts

programme.title: Maids for Sale
programme.desc: Undercover investigation exposing the people in Kuwait breaking local and international laws on modern slavery, including a woman offering a child for sale

programme.title: Emmanuelle in Soho (1981)

programme.desc: A widowed doctor travelling by train with his son realises that the mysterious driver has cut the brakes and they are speeding toward a fatal collision. programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: Drama and intrigue with the aristocrats and serving staff of 165 Eaton Place as it opens its doors once more. September 1938, and as dark clouds gather over Europe, Hallam's preoccupation with Nazi Germany leads him into dangerous waters overseas. Back home, Lady Agnes is frail following the birth of her second child, and finds great support in the forthright Aunt Blanche, who has made herself comfortable at Eaton Place following the funeral of her sister Lady Holland. In the servants' quarters, Rose is taken ill with TB, so the staff welcome spirited new maid Beryl, who catches Harry's eye, while events upstairs end in a startling revelation about Mr Pritchard. Keeley Hawes, Ed Stoppard, Alex Kingston and Adrian Scarborough star

programme.title: One Good Turn (1954)
programme.desc: A boy living in an orphanage grows up to be the institution's lovable but clumsy caretaker. He must save the children's home from a wicked property developer who wants to build a factory on the site, and tricks him into taking the kids to the seaside so he can knock the building down. Comedy, starring Norman Wisdom and Joan Rice

programme.title: The Cube
programme.desc: Boxer David Haye and I'm a Celebrity 2013 winner Kian Egan set out to complete a series of fiendishly difficult challenges, ranging from agility tests to skill trials, with the aim of winning a jackpot of £250,000 for charity. The more money they stand to win, the harder the task in front of them, but having both experienced the horrors of Bushtucker Trials, will they find it easier than most to hold their nerve? Phillip Schofield presents

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman put a selection of TV experts through their paces in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz show, asking them to team up with colleagues and contemporaries to provide the least-likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The House That £100K Built presenter Kieran Long teams up with fellow architecture specialist Piers Taylor, while Homes Under the Hammer host Martin Roberts joins forces with Selling Houses' Amanda Lamb. Fifth Gear duo Vicki Butler-Henderson and Tiff Needell combine their wits, and weather forecasters John Hammond and Laura Tobin also compete

programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present another special edition of the unorthodox general knowledge quiz show, pitting another set of celebrity duos against each other in a contest that sees them trying to guess th least likely correct answers to questions posed to members of the public.
